proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in black patients (feature_018=='black') than in white patients (feature_018=='white').
Mean pfs_months is lower in hispanic patients (feature_018=='hispanic') than in white patients (feature_018=='white').
Mean pfs_months differs across the five racial groups encoded by feature_018 (white, hispanic, black, asian, other) — i.e. there is at least one between-group difference in PFS.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(pfs[race=='black'], pfs[race=='white'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Black patients mean PFS 3.741 mo vs white 3.741 mo; Welch t-test t=-0.017, p=0.987. No evidence of a difference.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(pfs[race=='hispanic'], pfs[race=='white'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Hispanic patients mean PFS 3.737 mo vs white 3.741 mo; Welch t-test t=-0.144, p=0.886. No evidence of a difference.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.f_oneway over the five race groups
analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across 5 race groups gave p>0.5; pairwise group means cluster within ~0.01 mo of each other. No racial heterogeneity in PFS detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months is lower for medicaid patients (feature_045=='medicaid') than for privately insured patients (feature_045=='private').
Mean pfs_months is lower for uninsured patients (feature_045=='uninsured') than for privately insured patients (feature_045=='private').
Mean pfs_months is higher for medicare patients (feature_045=='medicare') than for privately insured patients (feature_045=='private').

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(pfs[insurance=='medicaid'], pfs[insurance=='private'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Medicaid mean PFS 3.741 vs private 3.736; Welch t=0.16, p=0.871. No difference.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(pfs[insurance=='uninsured'], pfs[insurance=='private'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Uninsured mean PFS 3.726 vs private 3.736; Welch t=-0.21, p=0.831. No difference.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(pfs[insurance=='medicare'], pfs[insurance=='private'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Medicare mean PFS 3.754 vs private 3.736; Welch t=0.91, p=0.362. Trend slightly positive but NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, suspected age) is associated with longer pfs_months — positive linear association.
PFS rises monotonically across quartiles of feature_078: Q1 patients have shortest PFS, Q4 patients longest PFS.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, sm.add_constant(df['feature_078'])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: each 1-unit increase in feature_078 raises PFS by 0.174 mo (p<1e-300). Per-decade effect = +1.74 mo. Strongly positive linear association — direction is opposite of typical 'older = worse outcome' pattern.

analyses.code: df.groupby(pd.qcut(df['feature_078'],4))['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Quartile means: Q1 (30-58) = 1.56 mo; Q2 (58-65) = 3.13 mo; Q3 (65-72) = 4.33 mo; Q4 (72-90) = 6.00 mo. Strict monotonic increase across all four quartiles — feature_078 is the single strongest continuous predictor of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lower feature_038 (continuous, range 6-18, suspected hemoglobin) is associated with shorter pfs_months — negative linear association expected if hgb anemia worsens PFS.
Across performance-status levels (feature_057, 0/1/2), pfs_months decreases as feature_057 increases (worse PS → shorter PFS).

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, sm.add_constant(df['feature_038'])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β = -0.0115 mo per unit feature_038, p=0.022. Direction is OPPOSITE the anemia hypothesis: higher hemoglobin associated with marginally shorter PFS. Effect dropped to NS in multivariable model (β=5.1e-5, p=0.95).

analyses.code: scipy.stats.f_oneway over feature_057 levels; OLS with feature_057 as continuous
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: PS0=4.68, PS1=3.49, PS2=2.38 (one-way ANOVA p<1e-300). Linear OLS slope = -1.16 mo per unit increase in feature_057. Strong monotonic negative association — feature_057 behaves exactly like ECOG performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher log(feature_013) (continuous, 0.12-3622, suspected PSA-like marker, log-skewed) is associated with shorter pfs_months — negative linear association.
Higher log(feature_092) (continuous, 48-830, suspected ALP-like marker) is associated with shorter pfs_months — negative linear association.
Higher feature_009 (continuous, 1.5-5.5, suspected serum albumin) is associated with longer pfs_months — positive linear association.
Higher feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is associated with shorter pfs_months — negative linear association.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, sm.add_constant(np.log(df['feature_013'].clip(lower=0.01)))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β = -0.348 mo per log-unit of feature_013, p<1e-300. Strong negative log-linear association — consistent with PSA-like prognostic marker.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, sm.add_constant(np.log(df['feature_092']))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β = -0.078 mo per log-unit feature_092, p=0.0027. After adjustment β=-0.101 (p<1e-100). Modest but robust negative association.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, sm.add_constant(df['feature_009'])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β = +0.495 mo per unit feature_009, p<1e-300. After full adjustment β=+0.478, p<1e-300. One of the strongest positive predictors — pattern matches serum albumin as a prognostic factor.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs_months, sm.add_constant(df['feature_006'])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β = -0.075 mo per unit feature_006, p<1e-30. Adjusted β = -0.077, p<1e-300. Strong negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_067 (range 6-10, suspected Gleason score) is associated with shorter pfs_months — negative linear association.
Among the remaining ordinal features (feature_016, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064), at least one is monotonically associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_067; one-way ANOVA across Gleason levels 6-10
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA p=0.37; linear OLS β=-0.006/level, p=0.49. Group means flat (3.71, 3.76, 3.74, 3.74, 3.70). NO association between feature_067 (Gleason-like) and PFS in this cohort.

analyses.code: Linear OLS for each ordinal feature: feature_016, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064
analyses.result_summary: All 6 ordinal features showed β between -0.01 and +0.004 with p>0.20. None is monotonically associated with PFS. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary indicator features, feature_051 (prevalence 55%) shows the strongest negative association with pfs_months (mean PFS lower when feature_051=1 than when feature_051=0).

After adjusting for log(feature_013) (suspected PSA), the marginal effect of feature_051 on pfs_months attenuates to near zero — i.e., feature_051 is essentially a PSA-stratification flag, not an independent prognostic.

analyses.code: Welch t-test PFS by each binary feature; rank by p-value
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 mean PFS = 3.51 mo; =0 mean = 4.03 mo; diff = -0.52 mo; Welch p=1.6e-181. Largest binary effect by far. Next strongest binary diffs are <0.10 mo.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006 + log(feature_013) + log(feature_092) + C(feature_057)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_051 β = -0.0018, p=0.65 — attenuates to null after controlling for log(feature_013). Among feature_051=1 patients log(PSA) mean = 3.50 vs 1.99 in feature_051=0. feature_051 is essentially a high-PSA flag.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_078, feature_009, feature_006, log feature_013, log feature_092), feature_109 (prev 10%) is positively associated with pfs_months — i.e., it acts like an effective treatment.
After the same multivariable adjustment, feature_039 (prev 10%) is positively associated with pfs_months — also treatment-like.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 + feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006 + log_f013 + log_f092 + C(feature_057)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β = +0.151 mo, p=4.3e-181. Apparent positive 'treatment effect' of feature_109. (See iter 14 — this signal is driven entirely by the f109+f039 dual-positive subgroup.)

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_039 + feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006 + log_f013 + log_f092 + C(feature_057)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted β = +0.149 mo, p=1.3e-179. Symmetrically positive to feature_109; same caveat — driven by the dual-positive subset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining 75 binary indicators, after multivariable adjustment, feature_027 (prev 60%) and feature_043 (prev 20%) show small but real (|β|<0.05 mo) associations with pfs_months.
All other binary features show |adjusted β| < 0.025 mo with p>0.05 — i.e., the predictive signal in the binary feature set is concentrated in feature_109/feature_039/feature_027/feature_043.

analyses.code: Iterate OLS pfs_months ~ binary + clinical covariates for each of 77 binary features
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 (60% prev): adjusted β=+0.025, p=6.1e-15. feature_043 (20% prev): adjusted β=-0.016, p=3.5e-5. Both small but highly significant in this large N.

analyses.code: Sort all 77 adjusted binary effects by p-value
analyses.result_summary: After feature_109/039/027/043, only feature_121 (β=+0.021, p=0.004), feature_089 (β=-0.009, p=0.008), feature_112 (β=+0.007, p=0.026) reach p<0.05; all with |β|<0.025. Bulk of binary signal is in the four leading features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_109 differs by race (feature_018) — i.e., proportion of patients with feature_109=1 varies meaningfully across white, black, hispanic, asian, other.
Receipt of feature_109 differs by insurance (feature_045) — i.e., medicaid/uninsured patients have lower receipt rates than privately insured.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_018')['feature_109'].mean(); chi-square
analyses.result_summary: Receipt rate of feature_109: white 10.04%, black 9.43%, hispanic 9.99%, asian 10.67%, other 9.72%. Range only 1.2 percentage points; no meaningful disparity.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_045')['feature_109'].mean(); chi-square
analyses.result_summary: Receipt of feature_109: private 9.87%, medicare 9.93%, medicaid 10.26%, uninsured 10.93%. If anything uninsured is slightly higher. No insurance-based disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs by race — specifically, the benefit is smaller in black or hispanic patients than in white patients (interaction term negative).
The treatment effect of feature_109 differs by insurance — benefit smaller for medicaid/uninsured than for private (interaction term negative).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * C(feature_018, Treatment(reference='white')) + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Within-race adjusted f109 effects: white +0.157 (p=2.7e-126), black +0.137 (p=2.3e-19), hispanic +0.138 (p=5.8e-24), asian +0.155 (p=2.2e-14), other +0.152 (p=1.1e-4). All four interaction p-values >0.19 vs white. Hypothesis of disparity REFUTED.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * C(feature_045, Treatment(reference='private')) + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction β for medicaid×f109 = +0.044 (p=0.006) — medicaid patients actually showed slightly LARGER f109 effect than privately insured. Medicare and uninsured interactions NS. Direction is opposite the disparity hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_109 differs by performance status (feature_057) — benefit attenuates in PS1 and PS2 vs PS0.
The treatment effect of feature_109 is larger in patients with lower log(feature_013) (low PSA) — i.e., negative interaction term feature_109:log_f013.
The treatment effect of feature_109 differs by feature_078 quartile.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * C(feature_057) + covariates; stratified models
analyses.result_summary: Stratified f109 β: PS0=+0.156, PS1=+0.140, PS2=+0.174 — all p<1e-22. Interaction p=0.14 (PS1) and 0.25 (PS2) vs PS0. Effect homogeneous across PS.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * log_f013_centered + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction β = -0.0067, p=0.082 — borderline negative trend (lower-PSA patients gain slightly more) but not significant at 0.05.

analyses.code: Stratified OLS by feature_078 quartile
analyses.result_summary: f109 β by quartile: Q1=+0.155, Q2=+0.141, Q3=+0.205, Q4=+0.178. Some heterogeneity (Q3 highest) but consistent positive direction across the full age range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_039 on pfs_months differs by race — smaller effect in black/hispanic than in white (negative interaction).

The marginal apparent benefit of feature_109 alone (vs no treatment) is essentially zero once feature_039 status is accounted for — i.e., feature_109's signal is mediated by co-occurrence with feature_039.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_039 * C(feature_018, Treatment(reference='white')) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Stratified f039 β: white +0.153, black +0.122, hispanic +0.149, asian +0.146, other +0.213. Largest gap is black-vs-white interaction p=0.056 (borderline). Disparity hypothesis weakly suggested but not significant.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ I(f109 & ~f039) + I(~f109 & f039) + I(f109 & f039) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Indicator coefficients: f109-only β=-0.0006 (p=0.91); f039-only β=+0.0005 (p=0.92); both β=+1.434 (p<1e-300, n=529). Confirms: alone, neither feature_109 nor feature_039 has any effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 are SYNERGISTIC: their multiplicative interaction term in an OLS model on pfs_months is large and positive (interaction β > +1.0 mo).
Among patients receiving BOTH feature_109 and feature_039 (dual-positive), mean pfs_months is at least 1.0 mo higher than among patients receiving neither.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_109 * feature_039 + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction β(feature_109:feature_039) = +1.434 mo, p<1e-300. Main effects of f109 and f039 both ≈0. Strong supra-additive synergy.

analyses.code: Group means by 2x2 cells of feature_109 x feature_039
analyses.result_summary: Cell means: neither (n=40435) 3.74; only_f109 (n=4467) 3.67; only_f039 (n=4569) 3.67; BOTH (n=529) 5.22. Dual-positive mean is 1.48 mo higher than neither.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic effect (dual-positive vs neither) is consistent across the three performance-status strata — i.e., the absolute benefit of dual treatment is similar at PS0, PS1, and PS2.
The synergistic effect of dual treatment differs by race — specifically, hispanic patients receive a smaller absolute benefit than white patients.

analyses.code: Cell means of f109 x f039 within each feature_057 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Dual−neither difference: PS0 = 6.37-4.66 = +1.71 mo (n_dual=183); PS1 = 4.82-3.50 = +1.32 mo (n_dual=259); PS2 = 3.99-2.36 = +1.63 mo (n_dual=87). Synergy persists across PS.

analyses.code: Cell means of f109 x f039 within each race stratum
analyses.result_summary: Dual−neither absolute differences: white +1.51 (n_dual=355), black +1.76 (n_dual=58), hispanic +0.99 (n_dual=78), asian +1.42 (n_dual=30), other not reported. Hispanic point estimate is lower but with wide CI; not formally significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of dual treatment (feature_109==1 AND feature_039==1) differs across race groups — i.e., chi-square test of dual receipt by feature_018 is significant.
Receipt of dual treatment differs across insurance groups (feature_045) — i.e., chi-square test of dual receipt by feature_045 is significant.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_018, dual))
analyses.result_summary: Dual receipt: white 1.10%, black 0.96%, hispanic 1.03%, asian 0.99%, other 0.82%. Chi-square=1.76, p=0.78. NO disparity in access to the synergistic combination by race.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_045, dual))
analyses.result_summary: Dual receipt: medicaid 1.10%, medicare 1.06%, private 1.02%, uninsured 1.20%. Chi-square=0.78, p=0.85. NO disparity in dual treatment receipt by insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of low feature_038 (anemia, defined as feature_038<11) on pfs_months is larger for black patients than for white patients — i.e., feature_038-low × race=black interaction is negative.
The negative effect of high log(feature_013) on pfs_months differs by race — specifically, the prognostic significance of PSA varies by race.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ hgb_low * C(feature_018, Treatment(reference='white')) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: hgb_low main β=+0.002 (p=0.73); hgb_low×black β=-0.022 (p=0.078); hgb_low×asian β=-0.005 (p=0.76); hgb_low×hispanic β=+0.002 (p=0.84). No significant disparity in anemia effect by race.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ log_f013_c * C(feature_018, Treatment(reference='white')) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Main log_f013 β=-0.349 (p<1e-300). Interactions with race: asian -0.008 (p=0.12), black +0.003 (p=0.40), hispanic -0.004 (p=0.26), other -0.008 (p=0.34). All NS. PSA prognostic strength is uniform across races.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The prognostic effect of feature_006 on pfs_months differs by race (negative interaction stronger in black/hispanic patients).
The age effect (feature_078 slope) on pfs_months differs by insurance — i.e., medicaid patients have a flatter or different age-pfs slope than private patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ f006_c * C(feature_018, Treatment(reference='white')) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Main β=-0.077 (p<1e-300). Race interactions: asian -0.0015, black -0.0020, hispanic -0.0005, other +0.0019; all p>0.10. No racial heterogeneity in feature_006 prognosis.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ f078_c * C(feature_045, Treatment(reference='private')) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Main β(feature_078)=+0.174 (p<1e-300). Interactions: medicaid×f078 = +0.001 (p=0.044, marginal); medicare and uninsured NS. Statistically marginal but |effect|≈+0.001/yr is clinically tiny.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_005 (continuous, range 6-22, mean 13) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_054 (continuous, range 80-195, possibly systolic BP) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_120 (continuous, range 20-508, possibly platelets) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β=+0.0049, p=0.20 (NS). No association.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_054
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β=+0.0011, p=0.079 (borderline). After adjustment NS.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_120
analyses.result_summary: Univariate β=+1.4e-5, p=0.83. No association with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Feature_027 (60% prevalent binary) shows a uniform, small positive effect on pfs_months across all five race groups (no race interaction).
Receipt of feature_027 differs by race or by insurance (chi-square significant).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 * C(feature_018) + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Main β=+0.026 (p<1e-30). Race interactions all p>0.5 (asian -0.0005, black -0.0043, hispanic -0.0005, other -0.0149). Uniform small benefit confirmed.

analyses.code: Group means + chi-square of feature_027 by race and by insurance
analyses.result_summary: Receipt of feature_027 by race: asian 59.6%, black 59.3%, hispanic 59.6%, white 60.1%, other 62.5% (range 3 pp). By insurance: medicaid 60.1%, medicare 59.9%, private 60.0%, uninsured 59.1% (range 1 pp). No meaningful disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic dual treatment (feature_109==1 & feature_039==1) provides positive incremental benefit on pfs_months above either monotherapy across all four feature_078 quartiles.

After full multivariable adjustment, the dual-treatment indicator (feature_109 * feature_039) remains the dominant binary predictor of pfs_months — coefficient larger than any other binary main or interaction term.

analyses.code: Group means of dual vs neither by feature_078 quartile (post-hoc check)
analyses.result_summary: Quartile means of dual-treated patients consistently exceed neither-treated cell means by 1.0–1.7 mo. Effect direction is uniform across the f078 range; numerical gap is largest in higher quartiles where baseline is higher. Synergy is robust to age stratum.

analyses.code: OLS with all key covariates plus f109:f039 interaction
analyses.result_summary: In the fully adjusted model the f109:f039 interaction β=+1.434 dwarfs every other binary predictor (next-largest |β|=0.026 for feature_027). Confirms dual-treatment receipt is the single most consequential binary predictor in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_018) has no detectable independent effect on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment for clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_078, feature_009, feature_006, log feature_013, log feature_092) — null hypothesis of equality across race groups holds.
Insurance (feature_045) has no detectable independent effect on pfs_months after multivariable adjustment for the same clinical predictors.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ C(feature_018, Treatment('white')) + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (all vs white): asian -0.004 (p=0.61), black +0.002 (p=0.69), hispanic +0.0001 (p=0.98), other -0.003 (p=0.80). All NS. Joint Wald test of race terms: p>0.85. No race effect after adjustment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ C(feature_045, Treatment('private')) + clinical covariates
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted insurance coefficients (vs private): medicaid -0.005 (p=0.38), medicare -0.001 (p=0.82), uninsured -0.003 (p=0.73). All NS. Joint test p>0.6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078), albumin-like feature_009, performance status feature_057, log feature_013 (PSA-like), and feature_006 are jointly the five strongest continuous/ordinal predictors of pfs_months in this cohort, and each remains highly significant in a single multivariable OLS together.
Hemoglobin-like feature_038 loses statistical significance after controlling for feature_057 and the other clinical predictors — i.e., its marginal univariate effect is confounded.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006 + log_f013 + log_f092 + C(feature_057) + feature_038
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted estimates (p<1e-100 for all unless noted): feature_078 β=+0.174/unit, feature_009 β=+0.478/unit, feature_006 β=-0.077/unit, log_f013 β=-0.349/log-unit, log_f092 β=-0.101/log-unit, PS1 β=-1.176, PS2 β=-2.286. Model R² ~0.85. All five primary predictors highly significant.

analyses.code: Same model — examine feature_038 coefficient
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_038 β=+5.1e-5 mo per unit, p=0.95. The univariate β=-0.012 (p=0.022) was driven entirely by confounding with PS/age/PSA. Hemoglobin has no independent prognostic information here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among rare binary features (prevalence <2%), at least one shows a significant effect on pfs_months after adjustment — i.e., feature_058 (1.9%), feature_004 (1.8%), feature_113 (2.0%), or feature_052 (2.0%) is significant.
Higher feature_009 (suspected albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months even within each performance-status stratum (no PS confounding of f009 effect).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS for each rare binary feature
analyses.result_summary: feature_058 β=+0.023, p=0.044 (just barely significant). feature_004, feature_113, feature_052 all p>0.20. The bulk of low-prevalence features are not informative; small samples limit power.

analyses.code: Stratified OLS pfs_months ~ feature_009 within each PS level
analyses.result_summary: Within PS0: f009 β≈+0.48; within PS1: ≈+0.47; within PS2: ≈+0.49. All p<1e-50. f009 effect is not confounded by performance status; it is an independent prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Overall conclusion (composite hypothesis): the strongest determinants of pfs_months in this cohort are, in order, feature_057 (PS), feature_078 (age-like), feature_009 (albumin-like), log feature_013 (PSA), feature_006, log feature_092 (ALP-like), and the dual-receipt of feature_109+feature_039 (synergistic combination treatment); there are NO significant disparities in PFS or in treatment receipt by race (feature_018) or insurance (feature_045).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006 + log_f013 + log_f092 + C(feature_057) + feature_109*feature_039 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)
analyses.result_summary: Final composite model recovers all main effects above with their reported magnitudes. f109:f039 interaction β=+1.43 (p<1e-300). All race and insurance coefficients have |β|<0.005 and p>0.4. Disparity hypotheses uniformly REFUTED. Synergistic dual treatment, performance status, and continuous prognostic markers fully explain the variance attributable to demographics in this cohort.
